meta.source: TradingView watchlist + FRED live scrape (Chrome extension) -- weekend pull, market data unchanged since Friday Jul 10 US cash close (markets closed Sat/Sun); watchlist edited by user since last pull -- enabling-layer chain tickers removed, DFII10 added

liquidity.label: Easing
decomp: No new H.4.1/RRP/SOFR prints since Friday's pull -- plumbing unchanged
Next H.4.1 release Jul 16; next RRP/SOFR prints Jul 13 (Monday)

credit.label: Stabilizing
decomp: ALLY -0.37%, CACC -0.77%, COF -0.14% into the close
MOVE +0.95% to 69.55, mild give-back after Friday's sharp drop

vol.label: Calming
decomp: MOVE +0.95%, mild reversal but still well off the elevated levels

confidence_meta.reason: CPI (Jul 14, 8:30am ET) is now ~41 hours away, inside the 48h binary-catalyst window -- applying the standard -8% penalty. MOVE (69.55) below the 80 threshold, no additional penalty there. Scores are otherwise unchanged from Saturday's pull, not split 2-2.

structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Quiet weekend re-check -- underlying market data unchanged from Friday's close (still no new session). Note: the enabling-layer chain is no longer tracked in the watchlist as of this run.
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: CPI print Jul 14, 8:30am ET (~41 hours away)
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW pass only (EWY below SMA20, KOSPI below 8,200)

bias: A quiet close to Friday's session -- plumbing metrics are unchanged since the prior pull (no new H.4.1/RRP/SOFR prints), and cash markets gave back a touch into the close after the midday liquidity-driven rally. VIX pressed to a fresh session low (15.02) even as credit-sensitive names (ALLY/CACC/COF) and the semis chain softened modestly. Nothing here changes the underlying thesis: conditions remain 'less restrictive, not accommodative' heading into next week's CPI print.

counterargs.thesis: Liquidity expansion / continued risk-on (unchanged from Friday's midday read)
supporting: Plumbing unchanged and still liquidity-positive: TGA down $106B/wk, reserves above $3T, RRP near zero
VIX pressed to a fresh session low (15.02, -5.24%)
USDJPY holding below the 162 BOJ threshold into the close
T10YIE/T5YIE breakevens still anchored, no sign of inflation re-acceleration
against: Credit-sensitive names (ALLY/CACC/COF) and the semis/enabling-layer chain gave back a touch into the close, a mild loss of Friday's earlier momentum
MOVE ticked up modestly (+0.95%) after its sharp drop earlier in the session
RRP buffer remains nearly exhausted (~$0.5B) -- no change, still a structural caveat
No new plumbing data until Monday (Jul 13) -- this run largely confirms rather than extends Friday's read
counterargs.read: This is essentially a confirmation pull at Friday's US cash close rather than a fresh macro signal -- the FRED plumbing series won't update again until Monday, and TradingView reflects the same session already captured this afternoon. The main takeaway is that the midday liquidity-driven rally faded slightly into the close (credit names and semis gave back modest gains), while equity vol kept compressing to a fresh low. None of this changes the broader liquidity-easing thesis; it's a quiet, lower-conviction end to the week heading into Monday's data-light session and next Tuesday's CPI print.

trade_table.rationale: Chain tickers (CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI, EOSE) were removed from the TradingView watchlist since the prior run -- no live data to score. Re-add to the watchlist to resume tracking.
Curve essentially flat into the close (10Y -0.4bp, 2Y +0.6bp). No fresh signal until next week's data.
All three softened modestly into the close after a strong week; HY OAS still flat at 2.70%. Not a reversal signal on its own.
Gate still closed 1/3 -- EWY ~13pts below SMA20, KOSPI ~725pts below the 8,200 trigger. USDKRW continuing to drift lower (1,498.87), furthest from the gate threshold yet.
Holding below 162 BOJ threshold (161.71) into the weekend; US-Japan 10Y spread steady at ~186bps.

catalysts.note: ~41 hours away, inside the 48h binary-catalyst window. Key input for the disinflation thesis given oil's recent volatility

duration.move_delta: +0.95%, mild give-back after the sharp drop

duration_score.classification: Quiet Close / Consolidation (plumbing unchanged into the weekend)
duration_score.reason: Curve essentially flat into Friday's close (10Y -0.4bp, 2Y +0.6bp, 30Y -1.1bp) with breakevens stable. No new plumbing data since the midday pull -- this is a low-conviction confirmation of the existing liquidity-easing thesis rather than a fresh signal.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Nothing new -- yields and breakevens both roughly flat into the close, real-rate pressure signal (DFII5 ~1.99%) essentially unchanged from midday.
james_framework.pain_trade: Not active this run. Flat curve, flat breakevens, calm credit.
james_framework.watch_signal: Watch for Monday's open and DFII5 into CPI (Jul 14) for the next real signal.

dfii5.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (DFII5)
dfii5.source: TradingView DFII5 — market-based, daily, direct TIPS pricing
dfii5.note: Essentially unchanged from midday (2.00%). Still no sustained decline -- real-rate pressure on high-beta/growth persists.

dfii10.label: 10Y TIPS real yield (DFII10)
dfii10.source: TradingView DFII10 -- market-based, daily, direct TIPS pricing. Newly added to the watchlist this run.
dfii10.note: Now live in the watchlist. Confirms DFII5's read -- both market-based real yields remain historically elevated, a structural headwind for long-duration growth/semis. Cross-checks closely against the derived US10Y - T10YIE calc (2.321%).

real_yield_primary.label: Primary real-rate pressure signal
real_yield_primary.note: Roughly flat this run. Watch for a sustained decline to confirm a genuine easing regime.

real_yield_10y_derived.note: Derived 10Y real yield, secondary confirmation. Use DFII5/DFII10 as primary.

two_y_proxy.label: 2Y modeled real-rate proxy
two_y_proxy.note: Essentially unchanged from midday (1.456%). Experimental secondary proxy -- DFII5 is the primary signal per James framework.

hierarchy.4: DGS2 − EXPINF2YR — Secondary modeled front-end proxy only

trading_interpretation.dfii5_falling: Supports high-beta growth / semis / DRAM / SOXX / QQQ
trading_interpretation.dfii5_rising_sharply: Pressure on growth, semis, long-duration assets
trading_interpretation.dfii5_above_prior_highs: Tightening regime risk — reduce high-beta exposure

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: T5YIE and T10YIE both anchored into the close. DFII5 roughly flat means the real-rate channel remains neutral -- neither easing nor tightening decisively this run.

chain_note: The enabling-layer thesis chain (CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI, EOSE) was removed from the TradingView watchlist since the prior pull -- only DRAM remains from that group, plus DFII10 was newly added. No price data available for the removed tickers this run; flag confirmed via direct watchlist inspection (symbol count dropped from 44 to 36, stock-sector mix narrowed from 4 sectors to 2). If this was unintentional, re-add the tickers to the TradingView watchlist before the next run.
